matched_terms: semantic consolidation; merge; no-merge; refactor; leg36; declaration consumption; consumer; public api
Goal: Semi-automated documentation of existing Lean code inside the same planning/proofbook tool.
Motivation: The Bestandscode cannot be fully normalized at once, but every module, declaration family, object, proof bridge, and consumer path can be documented with the same YAML/proof-dossier infrastructure so later interpretation is minimized.
Vision: Existing Lean-code documentation should grow into a reviewable object universe: each missing object/quantity becomes a dossier-backed node that can be inspected by the relevant expert without reading the full repository.
Raw data / tools: repo_inventory/raw_export modules.json, import_edges.json, symbol_reference_summary.json, dependency_order, mainline forensics, object register, proof dossiers, implementation scratchpad.
Operational scope: Secondary workflow: document existing code and its true consumers while Phase 1.x cleans the public API. It does not authorize refactors or deletion by itself.
Not-before gate: After Phase 1.1.1.1.4 declaration consumption classification and initial proof-dossier infrastructure are closed; deeper execution after Phase 1.1.5 import firewall is green.
Derived-only boundary: Documentation may classify provenance and consumers; it may not turn graph metadata into CNNA-generative assumptions.
Plan action: Add Phase 19.1 and object LEG16; use the generated Lean module graph to prevent forgotten code/documentation gaps. V1.12 adds LEG36 so existing-code documentation also records semantic consolidation/no-merge candidates before new substantive code.

Goal: Database-ready CNNA review and documentation structure
Motivation: A database-backed representation is likely the most effective path for querying, analyzing, visualizing, sharing, online display, interactive exploration, and discipline-separated expert review of the CNNA object world.
Vision: Long-term vision: a database-backed, queryable and online visualizable CNNA map - effectively an interactive semantic mindmap of the formal project - with speech/query instantiated context-docs, expert-review packets, and deep zoom from global structure to Lean sites. This is not current priority and remains secondary to the active Lean/content phases. The same future map should expose merge/no-merge refactor candidates and show where compactness would improve or damage provenance.
Raw data / tools: Single YAML master, generated TSV/JSON/DOT/PDF views, phase/object/proof-dossier records, module inventory, status legends, local theory context ledger, end-theory comparison ledger, future relational or graph database export.
Operational scope: Main side objective Phase 20. Technical database implementation is later and non-blocking, but schema discipline, expert-review semantics, and blue-audit obligations apply immediately.
Not-before gate: Principles apply immediately. Technical database export follows after YAML schema and generated views are stable enough for round-trip-safe identifiers and review links.
Derived-only boundary: The database is a documentation/review/query layer. It may expose, analyze, and visualize Lean/YAML-derived metadata, but it cannot supply CNNA-generative axioms, formulas, physics, stochasticity, or established-theory inputs.
Plan action: Add Phase 20 with DBR0-DBR5 objects; add GNG23-GNG24 and DB-REVIEW workflow; later implement schema/export/interactive views without changing Lean truth semantics.

Goal: Formal emergence-path forensics for open-system dynamics inside the derivation network.
Motivation: Later, once Pillar A/C objects are derived, the proof/module graph may help locate where closed finite subsystem assumptions become open effective dynamics through boundary/complement/regime handoffs.
Vision: When A/C open-system paths exist, graph forensics can show where regime, complement, boundary, and handoff structures make openness visible, but the dynamics must still be derived in Lean-facing CNNA objects.
Raw data / tools: A-regime profiles, complement-family profiles, DtN/Schur handoff modules, C/OQS consumer path, source-sink paths, dominator/cut analysis, object proof dossiers.
Operational scope: Post-core analysis of the formalization topology; not a replacement for deriving open-system behavior inside CNNA.
Not-before gate: After A regime/complement path, A->C handoff, and C OQS consumer roadmaps have enough checked objects to analyze. Treat as bonus/secondary until then.
Derived-only boundary: Emergence claims must still be proved from CNNA objects; graph forensics only localize proof paths, bottlenecks, and structural analogies.
Plan action: Add Phase 19.3 and object LEG18; keep as black/secondary target until the relevant A/C derivations exist.

Motivation: The IdealThread/full-cut extrema may later illuminate how one-dimensional recursive routes sit inside full boundary/operator geometry and how open-system behavior emerges from complement/interface structure.
Vision: Future string-analogy diagnostics may use derivation geometry as a comparison map around the thread/full-boundary extrema, only after the underlying route is derived and reviewed.
Raw data / tools: IdealThread/CutSpec.full profiles; import graph; derivation graph; Schur/DtN route audit; proof dossiers; source-sink paths and dominators.
Operational scope: Secondary diagnostic/comparison study only, after the underlying CNNA objects are code-derived and public-final.
Not-before gate: After S07, S08, BR06, BR07, S05/S06, and the relevant arithmetic/number-theory proof dossiers are closed.
Derived-only boundary: The diagnostic may compare formalization geometry to CNNA objects but cannot feed back as a generative axiom, formula, or shortcut.
Plan action: Keep as Phase 19.5 side-road; do not block active Phase 1 cleanup or Phase 3/4 neutralization.
